mission: Freeze the authoritative pharmacy directory, dispatch, Update Record, and manual fallback path model as a deterministic split between executable mock-now truth and fail-closed actual-provider-later access.

assumptions.summary: Seq_037 treats Service Search API version 3 as the primary live discovery candidate because it is the current version and EPS supplier guidance still points suppliers back to DoHS.
assumptions.consequence: Vecells can keep one strategic discovery direction while still inventorying legacy or urgent supporting routes separately.

assumptions.summary: Update Record remains a GP-visibility and consultation-summary route only. It does not replace dispatch transport, urgent return, safeguarding communication, or case closure.
assumptions.consequence: The matrix keeps discovery, dispatch, visibility, and urgent fallback as different truth domains.

assumptions.summary: Even after live digital routes exist, monitored NHSmail or professional phone fallback remains a real safety-net requirement for urgent return and disabled Update Record combinations.
assumptions.consequence: Manual fallback stays visible in the model instead of being treated as an embarrassing edge case.

official_guidance.title: Directory of Healthcare Services (Service Search) API
official_guidance.summary: The current catalogue positions Service Search API version 3 as the live version, with versions 1 and 2 deprecated on 2 February 2026. It supports searching by location together with clinical services, service type, and capability filters.
grounding: Service Search API version 3 is the current catalogue version.
Versions 1 and 2 were deprecated on 2 February 2026.
The route is directory search, not dispatch transport or closure proof.

official_guidance.title: Guide to search identifiers and service codes
official_guidance.summary: The guide publishes pharmacy-relevant identifiers and service codes including community pharmacy consultation and Pharmacy First codes, which makes route-level choice evidence and capability snapshots more concrete.
grounding: Pharmacy-related search identifiers are explicit, not inferred from free text.
Choice support must preserve the exact directory tuple and capability snapshot used.

official_guidance.title: Directory of Services - Urgent and Emergency Care - REST API
official_guidance.summary: The urgent-and-emergency-care REST API still sits in the official catalogue under the urgent-care taxonomy, while the Directory of Services overview keeps its primary use in NHS 111 and Pathways referral contexts. Vecells therefore treats it as watch or supporting directory context only, not as the baseline patient-choice route.
grounding: The route lives in urgent-and-emergency-care context.
The DoS service overview ties it to NHS 111 and urgent-care referral flows.
Urgent directory context does not make it the standard patient-choice route.

official_guidance.title: Electronic Prescription Service guidance for suppliers
official_guidance.summary: Current EPS supplier guidance says suppliers dispensing via EPS Release 2 require the Directory of Healthcare Services API and that it provides information on dispensing services available to prescribing systems. Vecells keeps EPS DoS visible only as a legacy supporting route and not as the main live discovery default.
grounding: DoHS remains explicitly required in current EPS supplier guidance.
The EPS-facing directory posture is supporting or legacy context, not a new strategic default.
Earlier blueprint migration law therefore remains directionally aligned with current official posture.

official_guidance.title: GP Connect: Update Record
official_guidance.summary: GP Connect Update Record remains the community-pharmacy route for pharmacist consultation summaries, with messages sent over MESH. The page also says Update Record is a silver service, so exact supplier and system combinations remain bounded and staged.
grounding: The route is for pharmacist consultation summaries, not generic pharmacy dispatch.
Silver-service posture means rollout is staged and assured-combination dependent.

official_guidance.title: GP Connect news
official_guidance.summary: Current GP Connect programme news still frames Update Record through Pharmacy First rollout and independent-pharmacy expansion, which confirms the route is live in principle but not a blanket assumption for every tenant or supplier combination.
grounding: Programme messaging is about staged rollout, not universal immediate reach.
Current rollout context reinforces the need for assured-combination checks and local fallback.

route_rows.actual_later_position: Open later once the directory access path, patient-choice compliance review, and live tuple evidence are signed off.
route_rows.official_status: Current official primary directory candidate for live pharmacy search.
route_rows.official_version_posture: Service Search API v3 current; versions 1 and 2 deprecated on 2 February 2026.
route_rows.consent_dependency: Choice list may be shown without consent, but provider binding, reassurance, and dispatch require an active PharmacyConsentCheckpoint on the same visible-choice tuple.
route_rows.provider_capability_snapshot_requirement: One current PharmacyDirectorySnapshot plus the matching PharmacyProviderCapabilitySnapshot set feeding the current PharmacyChoiceProof.
route_rows.dispatch_proof_requirement: None directly. Directory evidence cannot stand in for PharmacyDispatchPlan, PharmacyDispatchAttempt, or DispatchProofEnvelope.
route_rows.acknowledgement_or_expiry_semantics: Snapshot freshness, visible-choice-set hash drift, and choice-session expiry supersede earlier selection and consent bindings.
route_rows.ambiguity_mode: Stale or changed directory tuples force same-shell regeneration and may invalidate a prior choice.
route_rows.degraded_fallback_mode: Same-shell directory regeneration, warned choice, or clinician/manual fallback when no safe provider remains.
route_rows.closure_blocker_implications: Directory results cannot resolve or close the request lineage and cannot imply that a referral was sent.
inspector_notes: Simulate snapshot freshness, provider opening hours, provider withdrawal, no-safe-choice conditions, warned choices, and consent reset when the selected provider or pathway drifts.
Current PharmacyDirectorySnapshot and PharmacyChoiceProof over the visible choice set and selected provider explanation.
Patient-visible provider order depends on PharmacyChoiceProof and selectionBindingHash.
source_refs: blueprint/phase-6-the-pharmacy-loop.md#6C. Pharmacy discovery, provider choice, and directory abstraction

route_rows.route_label: DoS UEC REST / watch or supporting route
route_rows.actual_later_position: Use only after a bounded urgent-care architecture decision if the route remains necessary.
route_rows.official_status: Urgent-care directory context only; not the baseline patient-choice route.
route_rows.official_version_posture: Official urgent-and-emergency-care REST API remains catalogued in production context.
route_rows.consent_dependency: Watch lookups do not satisfy pharmacy-choice consent and may not authorise dispatch or reassurance.
route_rows.provider_capability_snapshot_requirement: If used at all, results must still normalize into the current PharmacyProviderCapabilitySnapshot set and may not bypass PharmacyChoiceProof.
route_rows.dispatch_proof_requirement: None. Directory context is separate from transport truth.
route_rows.acknowledgement_or_expiry_semantics: Route freshness matters only for supporting urgent-care awareness; it does not create a dispatch deadline.
route_rows.ambiguity_mode: A route may indicate urgent-care context while still telling Vecells nothing about provider choice or dispatch proof.
route_rows.degraded_fallback_mode: Escalate to the monitored manual urgent-return route if urgent professional contact is required.
route_rows.closure_blocker_implications: Urgent directory awareness cannot settle the pharmacy case or justify calm copy.
inspector_notes: Keep urgent directory context separate from patient-visible pharmacy choice.
Do not let urgent directory access masquerade as a send route.
source_refs: blueprint/phase-6-the-pharmacy-loop.md#6G. Bounce-back, urgent return, and reopen mechanics

route_rows.actual_later_position: Stay legacy-supporting only, and never outrank the Service Search v3 route without a fresh policy decision.
route_rows.official_status: Legacy or supporting route only; not the strategic live default.
route_rows.official_version_posture: Current EPS-facing supplier guidance still points suppliers at DoHS; keep EPS DoS only as a supporting posture.
route_rows.consent_dependency: No standalone consent authority. Supporting data may inform choice only after policy acceptance and tuple normalization.
route_rows.provider_capability_snapshot_requirement: Normalize any EPS-facing service detail into the same PharmacyProviderCapabilitySnapshot and PharmacyChoiceProof set.
route_rows.dispatch_proof_requirement: None. Directory evidence is not transport proof.
route_rows.acknowledgement_or_expiry_semantics: Legacy directory freshness may invalidate a supporting hint, but it never refreshes a frozen choice silently.
route_rows.ambiguity_mode: Legacy route data can become stale or policy-incompatible and must not silently reorder a patient choice.
route_rows.degraded_fallback_mode: Drop back to Service Search v3 or explicit same-shell re-selection if the supporting tuple drifts.
route_rows.closure_blocker_implications: Legacy directory hints never close the case and may not justify quiet success.
inspector_notes: Keep EPS DoS visible so deprecation and migration debt stay explicit.
Do not let legacy data outrank the current visible choice tuple.
source_refs: blueprint/phase-6-the-pharmacy-loop.md#6C. Pharmacy discovery, provider choice, and directory abstraction

route_rows.actual_later_position: Open later only after the dispatch route, proof thresholds, and manual-assisted redispatch policy are frozen.
route_rows.official_status: Live route not yet selected; current truth is simulator-first only.
route_rows.official_version_posture: Transport posture remains bounded to simulator law now; seq_028 currently inventories 10 MESH route rows separately.
route_rows.consent_dependency: Requires the current PharmacyConsentCheckpoint, selected provider, pathway, package fingerprint, and selectionBindingHash.
route_rows.provider_capability_snapshot_requirement: One selected PharmacyProviderCapabilitySnapshot plus the live dispatch adapter binding and TransportAssuranceProfile.
route_rows.dispatch_proof_requirement: Current PharmacyDispatchPlan, PharmacyDispatchAttempt, DispatchProofEnvelope, and ExternalConfirmationGate on the same tuple.
route_rows.acknowledgement_or_expiry_semantics: Transport accepted, provider accepted, proof pending, proof deadline, expiry, and redispatch are all separate facts.
route_rows.ambiguity_mode: Proof pending, contradiction, or consent drift holds the same shell in pending or recovery posture.
route_rows.degraded_fallback_mode: Controlled redispatch under a fresh tuple, or manual-assisted dispatch only where policy permits.
route_rows.closure_blocker_implications: Transport acceptance or mailbox pickup cannot calm the patient or close the pharmacy lineage.
route_rows.freshness_label: tuple must stay frozen through proof window
inspector_notes: Simulate dispatch attempts, transport accepted versus authoritative proof, proof deadlines, redispatch, weak email or weak mailbox returns, urgent return phone escalation, and reopen-for-safety. Update Record may not stand in for urgent return and mock send acceptance may not count as resolved dispatch truth.
Update Record may not stand in for urgent return or dispatch confirmation.
Weak transport proof blocks calmness and closure.
source_refs: blueprint/phase-6-the-pharmacy-loop.md#6D. Referral pack composer, dispatch adapters, and transport contract

route_rows.route_label: GP Connect Update Record / assured path
route_rows.actual_later_position: Open later only when the community-pharmacy supplier or GP-system combination is named, assured, and replay-safe.
route_rows.official_status: Assured visibility and consultation-summary path only.
route_rows.official_version_posture: Current GP Connect Update Record route for consultation summaries over MESH; silver-service rollout remains combination dependent.
route_rows.consent_dependency: Cannot replace dispatch consent. Outcome observation must still bind the selected provider, pathway, scope hash, and active case tuple.
route_rows.provider_capability_snapshot_requirement: The current selected-provider snapshot, outcome source binding, and PharmacyOutcomeReconciliationGate policy must all match.
route_rows.dispatch_proof_requirement: None directly. Update Record never proves that the referral was sent or that urgent escalation is satisfied.
route_rows.acknowledgement_or_expiry_semantics: MESH or inbox delivery is supporting transport evidence only; duplicates, replays, disabled routes, and weak matches remain case-local.
route_rows.update_record_eligibility_scope: Consultation summary and GP visibility only; never urgent return, safeguarding, or generic referral transport.
route_rows.ambiguity_mode: Weak match, duplicate, replay, partial summary, or disabled practice route keeps the case in reconciliation or recovery.
route_rows.degraded_fallback_mode: Use practice-disabled manual visibility fallback and keep the reconciliation gate open.
route_rows.closure_blocker_implications: Observed summaries or transport receipts do not auto-close the request; only explicit reconciliation resolution may unblock closure.
route_rows.freshness_label: assured combination and current case tuple required
inspector_notes: Simulate exact replay, semantic replay, collision review, unresolved match, resolved_apply, resolved_reopen, unmatched, proof-bearing correlation chains, and blocked closure. Weakly matched outcomes may not auto-close the request or stand in for authoritative proof.
Vecells does not write directly into the GP record.
Outcome arrival and closure truth are separate decisions.
source_refs: blueprint/phase-6-the-pharmacy-loop.md#6F. Outcome ingest, Update Record observation, and reconciliation
blueprint/phase-6-the-pharmacy-loop.md#6G. Bounce-back, urgent return, and reopen mechanics

route_rows.actual_later_position: Remain a supporting dependency only after live mailbox, workflow, or equivalent transport specifics are frozen.
route_rows.official_status: Supporting dependency only; not business truth.
route_rows.official_version_posture: Current MESH and secure-transport posture remains separately inventoried from the pharmacy route decision.
route_rows.consent_dependency: Follows the existing dispatch or observation tuple; it cannot establish consent, provider choice, or calmness.
route_rows.provider_capability_snapshot_requirement: Bound to the active dispatch plan or outcome-source adapter, not to directory ranking.
route_rows.dispatch_proof_requirement: Supporting receipts only. Mailbox acceptance or pickup cannot satisfy DispatchProofEnvelope on its own.
route_rows.acknowledgement_or_expiry_semantics: Pickup, delayed acknowledgement, non-delivery, or replay are supporting transport facts and remain time-bound.
route_rows.ambiguity_mode: Message accepted, picked up, or replayed without the right case correlation leaves the route non-authoritative.
route_rows.degraded_fallback_mode: Escalate to manual urgent or manual visibility fallbacks if mailbox or secure transport becomes unreliable.
route_rows.closure_blocker_implications: Transport dependency signals cannot close the case or downgrade a weak match into resolved truth.
inspector_notes: Transport accepted, picked up, and reconciled are different facts.
Keep workflow and mailbox governance tied to seq_028.

route_rows.route_label: Manual NHSmail or phone / urgent fallback
route_rows.actual_later_position: Remain mandatory as the urgent safety-net even after live digital routes exist.
route_rows.official_status: Local monitored safety-net route; not a digital API.
route_rows.official_version_posture: Runbook current, practice or tenant owned.
route_rows.consent_dependency: Urgent safety handling does not rely on patient-choice consent, but it must still bind the active PharmacyCase and duty route.
route_rows.provider_capability_snapshot_requirement: Current UrgentReturnChannelConfig, case identifiers, and local owner or duty-contact binding.
route_rows.dispatch_proof_requirement: Human acknowledgement or explicit duty handoff evidence only; sending an email or starting a call is not enough.
route_rows.acknowledgement_or_expiry_semantics: Manual acknowledgement windows, overdue escalation, and missed-safety-net recovery are explicit.
route_rows.update_record_eligibility_scope: Not applicable and explicitly separate from Update Record.
route_rows.ambiguity_mode: Email sent, voicemail left, or phone transfer attempted without acknowledgement remains unresolved.
route_rows.degraded_fallback_mode: Escalate immediately to duty task, supervisor, or practice-contact recovery.
route_rows.closure_blocker_implications: Urgent return evidence reopens or blocks closure until acknowledged and clinically reconciled.
route_rows.freshness_label: owner rehearsal and monitored route required
inspector_notes: Urgent return is a professional-contact route, not an Update Record side effect.
This route remains current even if live APIs onboard later.
source_refs: blueprint/phase-6-the-pharmacy-loop.md#6G. Bounce-back, urgent return, and reopen mechanics

route_rows.actual_later_position: Remain explicit for practices or combinations where Update Record is disabled, unsupported, or delayed.
route_rows.official_status: Manual visibility fallback when Update Record cannot be relied on.
route_rows.official_version_posture: Local fallback contract, not an NHS API version.
route_rows.consent_dependency: The active consent, provider, and outcome tuple still govern; the fallback route does not rewrite choice or dispatch truth.
route_rows.provider_capability_snapshot_requirement: Current PracticeVisibilityFallbackPlan together with the selected provider and active case tuple.
route_rows.dispatch_proof_requirement: None for dispatch. This route provides manual visibility acknowledgement only.
route_rows.acknowledgement_or_expiry_semantics: Manual visibility acknowledgement is required and can itself become overdue or wrong-recipient recovery work.
route_rows.update_record_eligibility_scope: Explicit alternative when Update Record is disabled or unsupported.
route_rows.ambiguity_mode: Manual note sent without acknowledgement or sent to the wrong practice route remains unresolved.
route_rows.degraded_fallback_mode: Keep the same shell in recovery and escalate through staff operations if practice visibility is still missing.
route_rows.closure_blocker_implications: Manual visibility proof cannot auto-close the case and cannot turn a weak outcome into calm success.
inspector_notes: Practice-disabled Update Record must be explicit so the team does not quietly assume visibility exists.
Manual visibility still cannot auto-close the request.
source_refs: blueprint/phase-6-the-pharmacy-loop.md#6F. Outcome ingest, Update Record observation, and reconciliation
blueprint/phase-6-the-pharmacy-loop.md#6H. Practice visibility, operations queue, and pharmacy exception handling

update_record_rows.path_label: GP Connect Update Record / assured path
update_record_rows.supported_scope: consultation summary and GP visibility only
update_record_rows.assured_combination_requirement: named community-pharmacy professional plus assured supplier or GP-system combination
update_record_rows.correlation_requirement: must correlate to the active PharmacyCase and outcome gate
update_record_rows.duplicate_or_replay_policy: duplicate or replay remains case-local reconciliation work
update_record_rows.closure_policy: never auto-close from transport or weak match
update_record_rows.source_refs: blueprint/phase-6-the-pharmacy-loop.md#6F. Outcome ingest, Update Record observation, and reconciliation; official_gp_connect_update_record

update_record_rows.transport_dependency: MESH, mailbox, or equivalent secure transport
update_record_rows.assured_combination_requirement: depends on the paired update-record or transport route
update_record_rows.correlation_requirement: supporting receipts must still bind to the active case tuple
update_record_rows.duplicate_or_replay_policy: message acceptance or replay is non-authoritative without reconciliation
update_record_rows.closure_policy: cannot close or reassure by itself

update_record_rows.supported_scope: manual GP visibility when Update Record is disabled or unavailable
update_record_rows.correlation_requirement: must bind to the active case and selected provider tuple
update_record_rows.duplicate_or_replay_policy: manual duplicate or wrong-recipient handling stays explicit
update_record_rows.closure_policy: no auto-close and no calmness from manual visibility alone
update_record_rows.source_refs: blueprint/phase-6-the-pharmacy-loop.md#6H. Practice visibility, operations queue, and pharmacy exception handling; official_gp_connect_programme_news

update_record_rows.path_label: Manual NHSmail or phone / urgent fallback
update_record_rows.supported_scope: urgent return or professional safety-net contact
update_record_rows.correlation_requirement: must bind to the active PharmacyCase or reopened request lineage
update_record_rows.duplicate_or_replay_policy: attempted contact without acknowledgement remains unresolved
update_record_rows.closure_policy: urgent return evidence blocks closure until acknowledged and reconciled
update_record_rows.source_refs: blueprint/blueprint-init.md#7. Pharmacy First pathway; blueprint/phase-6-the-pharmacy-loop.md#6G. Bounce-back, urgent return, and reopen mechanics

proof_ladders.title: Discovery and choice proof
steps: Resolve one current directory source tuple and normalize it into PharmacyDirectorySnapshot.
Materialize the matching PharmacyProviderCapabilitySnapshot set and explicit visible-choice frontier.
Mint PharmacyChoiceProof over the visible-choice-set hash and any warnings or suppressed providers.
Require a fresh choice or consent renewal if the tuple drifts before dispatch.
proof_ladders.ui_rule: Choice explanation may appear only while the current PharmacyChoiceProof and visible-choice-set hash remain valid.

proof_ladders.title: Dispatch proof and expiry
steps: Freeze PharmacyReferralPackage on the same selected-provider and consent tuple.
Compile PharmacyDispatchPlan with one live TransportAssuranceProfile and adapter binding.
Record PharmacyDispatchAttempt and keep transport acceptance, provider acceptance, and authoritative proof distinct.
Hold patient or staff calmness until DispatchProofEnvelope and ExternalConfirmationGate both satisfy the route policy.
proof_ladders.ui_rule: Transport accepted is pending evidence, not final referred truth.

proof_ladders.title: Visibility and reconciliation proof
steps: Observe the inbound summary or supporting transport event on the active case tuple.
Separate transport receipt from clinical or operational outcome evidence.
Run duplicate, replay, weak-match, and wrong-route checks through PharmacyOutcomeReconciliationGate.
Allow calm closure only after a resolved apply or resolved reopen outcome under the current gate.
proof_ladders.ui_rule: Observed summaries and mailbox events may inform status, but they never auto-close the case.

proof_ladders.title: Manual fallback and urgent recovery proof
steps: Bind one monitored human-owned route to the active case and route intent.
Capture explicit acknowledgement or duty handoff evidence.
Escalate immediately if acknowledgement is overdue, misrouted, or clinically unsafe.
Keep closure blocked until the manual acknowledgement is reconciled with the active pharmacy lineage.
proof_ladders.ui_rule: Manual fallback is continuity protection, not success.

gap_rows.summary: The live Service Search access path, certificate or credential posture, and tenancy overlays are not yet onboarded.
gap_rows.next_step: Carry directory onboarding through later provider access tasks before any live claim is made.

gap_rows.summary: EPS-facing supporting posture remains legacy debt and must not silently become the main directory story.
gap_rows.next_step: Keep the route watch-only until a separate policy review says otherwise.
source_refs: blueprint/phase-6-the-pharmacy-loop.md#6C. Pharmacy discovery, provider choice, and directory abstraction

gap_rows.summary: The live referral dispatch route, proof thresholds, and manual-assisted redispatch posture are not yet frozen.
gap_rows.next_step: Complete the live transport selection and assurance-profile review before any provider claim.
source_refs: blueprint/phase-6-the-pharmacy-loop.md#6D. Referral pack composer, dispatch adapters, and transport contract

gap_rows.summary: Seq_037 does not yet name the exact assured community-pharmacy and GP-system combination for Update Record.
gap_rows.next_step: Record the named combination and environment before any live visibility claim.

gap_rows.summary: The monitored mailbox or professional phone owner chain is not yet recorded per live tenant.
gap_rows.next_step: Freeze ownership and rehearse acknowledgement escalation before claiming a live urgent route.

gap_rows.summary: The manual visibility runbook exists conceptually, but the per-practice rehearsal evidence is not yet current.
gap_rows.next_step: Exercise the disabled-Update-Record branch before allowing quiet operational assumptions.
source_refs: blueprint/phase-6-the-pharmacy-loop.md#6H. Practice visibility, operations queue, and pharmacy exception handling

gap_rows.summary: Supporting mailbox or workflow posture remains a watch dependency and cannot be treated as settled transport proof.
gap_rows.next_step: Keep seq_028 mailbox or workflow governance tied to the pharmacy route decision before any live opening.

gap_rows.summary: Weak-match, duplicate, and replay handling remain blocked until outcome parser quality and manual-review thresholds are signed off.
gap_rows.next_step: Freeze parser quality and manual-review policy before any live summary ingestion claim.
source_refs: blueprint/phase-6-the-pharmacy-loop.md#6F. Outcome ingest, Update Record observation, and reconciliation

decisions.title: Service Search v3 is the primary live discovery candidate.
decisions.rationale: Use the current Service Search v3 posture as the main live discovery direction while keeping EPS-facing legacy support visible but subordinate.

decisions.title: Urgent-care DoS stays watch-only or supporting.
decisions.rationale: Urgent and emergency care directory context matters, but it is not the baseline patient-visible pharmacy-choice route.

decisions.title: Dispatch transport is its own truth domain.
decisions.rationale: Discovery, transport acceptance, business acknowledgement, and authoritative dispatch proof remain separate facts.

decisions.title: Update Record is visibility and outcome observation only.
decisions.rationale: GP Connect Update Record supports consultation summaries and GP visibility, not urgent return, safeguarding, or referral transport.

decisions.title: Manual urgent fallback stays explicit.
decisions.rationale: A monitored NHSmail or professional phone route remains necessary for urgent return and cannot be hidden behind digital optimism.
source_refs: blueprint/phase-6-the-pharmacy-loop.md#6G. Bounce-back, urgent return, and reopen mechanics

decisions.title: Outcome observation never auto-closes the request.
decisions.rationale: Weak matches, duplicates, delayed summaries, and disabled routes keep the PharmacyOutcomeReconciliationGate explicit and closure-blocking.
source_refs: blueprint/phase-6-the-pharmacy-loop.md#6F. Outcome ingest, Update Record observation, and reconciliation
blueprint/forensic-audit-findings.md#Finding 79 - weak-source matching did not clearly stop at a case-local review state

decisions.title: Actual-provider progression remains fail-closed.
decisions.rationale: Live routes remain blocked until MVP, approver, environment, mutation flag, watch-register clearance, and route-specific assurance evidence all exist.

live_gates.title: Pharmacy provider scorecards are approved
live_gates.summary: Seq_022 already froze the pharmacy directory, transport, and outcome scorecard families.

live_gates.title: Transport scorecards and message-route evidence are approved
live_gates.summary: Seq_028 already froze message-route and proof separation for pharmacy-related MESH posture.

live_gates.title: Credible pharmacy MVP is approved
live_gates.summary: Actual-provider work stays blocked until a bounded pharmacy MVP is explicitly named and approved.

live_gates.title: Consent and dispatch proof models are implemented
live_gates.summary: The canonical contracts are specified, but seq_037 does not claim runtime implementation is already complete.
source_refs: blueprint/phase-6-the-pharmacy-loop.md#6D. Referral pack composer, dispatch adapters, and transport contract

live_gates.title: Named approver and target environment are present
live_gates.summary: Real-provider route discovery remains fail-closed until a named approver and environment target exist.

live_gates.title: ALLOW_REAL_PROVIDER_MUTATION is true
live_gates.summary: Real provider mutation must remain blocked unless ALLOW_REAL_PROVIDER_MUTATION=true is supplied deliberately.

live_gates.title: Gap and watch register is clear for the attempted route
live_gates.summary: The current assurance gaps still include unresolved blockers across discovery, transport, urgent fallback, and outcome observation.

live_gates.title: Update Record assured combination is named
live_gates.summary: GP Connect Update Record remains blocked until the exact assured combination and rollout context are recorded.

live_gates.title: Urgent return ownership and rehearsal are current
live_gates.summary: The manual safety-net route is mandatory, but live opening stays blocked until ownership and rehearsal evidence are recorded.

live_gates.title: Phase 0 external foundation gate is open
live_gates.summary: Seq_020 still reports Phase 0 entry as withheld, so actual provider motion cannot be treated as current-baseline execution.

truth_proof_digest: Current PharmacyDirectorySnapshot and PharmacyChoiceProof over the visible choice set and selected provider explanation.
Current DispatchProofEnvelope(authoritative proof satisfied) accepted by the live TransportAssuranceProfile and current ExternalConfirmationGate for the same dispatch attempt.
Current urgent-return or bounce-back evidence acknowledged on the configured route and bound to the active PharmacyCase or reopened request lineage.
Current PharmacyOutcomeRecord or PharmacyBounceBackRecord correlated to the active PharmacyCase and, where needed, resolved through PharmacyOutcomeReconciliationGate.

directory.focus: directory snapshot freshness, opening hours, warned-choice states, no-safe-choice conditions, selection binding hashes, and consent reset on drift

dispatch.focus: frozen referral package, dispatch plan and attempt tuples, proof envelopes, urgent-return channels, redispatch, and weak-transport ambiguity

outcome.focus: Update Record observation, replay-safe ingest, weak-match gates, manual review, unresolved outcomes, and reopen-for-safety branches

eps_supplier_guidance: Directory of Healthcare Services (Service Search) API
